Hi Matthew,

Thank you for getting in touch with this opportunity.

Could you let me know the cost associated with event involvement, please? Then I'll put you in touch with the right person to get more info.

Kind regards,
Emily


Emily Cotter (she/her)

Hi Sarah,


Thanks for taking my call there. I'm reaching out from the media partner for the Students' Union at the University of Leeds (LUU). We'd like to share details with your team on our "Feel Good: Health" fairs which are taking place in March and April on campus this year.


We are undertaking a significant brand activation with the NHS in Leeds this year who will be at these events and we'd love to see if Leeds Mind can also attend.


There is a spend to be involved on campus events, however we do offer significant reductions for charities, funded organisations, or CICs to ensure they are as accessible as possible.


Please let me know if there is any availability in the diaries for an introductory Zoom call with me for 15-20 minutes, as I'd be delighted to share more information.


Best,


Matt Billington (he/him)
University Bookings Manager, Media & Events

subject: Re: Pen to Paper - Refreshers Fair
body_text:
Hi Aaron,

Apologies, this has all happened during our year end and getting any spare
moments during that is almost impossible. I am sad to hear that the
refreshers fair is being cancelled. I read through the documents you sent
me, thanks. I feel their focus is not where we operate and hence I don't
think we would benefit from attending those.

If you have any other events, please do send me information. Otherwise, if
you just refund the payment, we can look at Freshers Fair this October.

Do you deal with the University of York Freshers Fair too?

Cheers

Grae

Hi Aaron,

Apologies, this has all happened during our year end and getting any spare moments during that is almost impossible. I am sad to hear that the refreshers fair is being cancelled. I read through the documents you sent me, thanks. I feel their focus is not where we operate and hence I don't think we would benefit from attending those. 

If you have any other events, please do send me information. Otherwise, if you just refund the payment, we can look at Freshers Fair this October.

Do you deal with the University of York Freshers Fair too?

Cheers

Grae

On Saturday, 31 January 2026, 12:51:55 GMT, Caitlin Small <liverpool@pixel-bar.co.uk> wrote:
>Hey Indy,
>
>Thanks for getting back to us on this! I really appreciate you lowering the price, I will be chatting to our directors over the weekend and I'll be able to let you know next week if that's okay with you? 🙂
>
>Thanks,
>________________________________
>From: Inderpal gill <Inderpalgill@nativecampusadvertising.com>
>Sent: 30 January 2026 4:09 PM
>To: Pixel Bar Liverpool <liverpool@pixel-bar.co.uk>
>Cc: Edward Ta <Edward@pixel-bar.co.uk>; Lee Davies <Lee@pixel-bar.co.uk>
>Subject: Re: Entertainment, Leisure & Hospitality Fair - JMU - February 16th
>
>
>Heya,
>
>
>Sorry for the delay in response - been sitting with the university representative team at JMU today and I covered the pricing issue we've encountered here.
>
>
>I can move forward to further discount the stall to the floor price listed within our current contractual agreement with JMU for all fairs - £210. They are processing a review of their pricing model after multiple advertisers have highlighted similar concerns.
>
>
>Also to mention, with the booking of a stall at any point over the academic year, this permits an advertiser to receive Freshers booking information prior to general sale - as a sign of good will from Native/JMU to all advertisers who come onto campus.
>
>
>Would that be something you would want to move ahead with?
>
>
>Best,
>
>Indy
>

I can move forward to further discount the stall to the floor price listed within our current contractual agreement with JMU for all fairs - £210. They are processing a review of their pricing model after multiple advertisers have highlighted similar concerns.

subject: Native x Clue
body_text:
Hi Hugo, 



Hope you are well and had a great weekend. 



Thank you very much for your enquiry with Native and how we can help Clue shine in front of students. 



Native is the UK’s largest student engagement platform. We connect brands like yours with 2.9 million students across 55+ university campuses through the UK’s biggest and most high-impact student events. 



Would you be free for an online meeting this week with our lovely campaign strategist? 



Look forward to speaking with you soon! 



Kind Regards 

Sheraaz Ahmed

Look forward to speaking with you soon!

Hey Natalie,


Yep all good here, hope all is well with you!


So the digital screens are located in specific universities, so we'd just need you to confirm which universities you'd like to target. We need a two week lead time to get you up on the screens, and then your advert appears for a fortnight, so we've still got time to work around you.


For the greatest reach, I'd recommend Queen Mary's - if you can get £1000 approved (so covering 5 screens), you would then appear on all the screens in that university. Queen Mary's has the most number of screens (26) and also one of the largest student bodies in London - so this would put you in front of the most eyeballs.


If you wanted to target the most creative students, I'd recommend Goldsmiths. It has 10 screens and strong links to creative production and industries.


Or if you wanted to do one screen at a number of different universities each, this is also possible.


Thanks,


Jack

Hi Natalie,


Good, I'm glad it was useful!


And in terms of added digital promotion - I've copied in some information below. I'm also happy to jump on a meeting with you, if you'd like more information about how these work, and to talk you through them.


Just let me know and we can get some time in the calendar.


Jack


//


Solus Emails

If you want to tell a real story, our solus emails are the way to go. You can use as many words, images, and links as you like, in a HTML file totally dedicated to your business! We can hit about 65,000 London contacts on our database, and even more through the student unions. We typically see a 31% open rate and a 1% CTR. I’ve attached a doc with more details for you.


Cost -  These are 10p per send with a two-week lead time. For all 65,000 London emails send £6500 +VAT, and we can scale this up or down to suit your needs.


Social Media

We can also run promotional posts across Instagram (Stories and Feed) and Facebook. We can target these geographically anywhere in the UK, but also specifically to students in London.


Here are the packages:

- 100k impressions: £1050 + VAT

- 200k impressions: £2000 + VAT

- 300k impressions: £2800 + VAT

- 400k impressions: £3700 + VAT

- 500k impressions: £4500 + VAT


If you're interested, just let me know the [URL/Link], [@Tag], and [Copy - 200 chars max] you'd like to use.


Digital Screens

Our screens are live during term time and are great for getting noticed. These get you a 10-second slot every minute alongside our organic content. We just need a two-week lead time and your creative in MP4 or JPEG format. I’ve attached the full list of available university screens for you to check out.


Cost - It’s £200 + VAT per screen for a two-week block. Our screens are capped at £1000 - so for example if you wanted to target Goldsmiths, you could appear on all 10 screens for the cost of 5 (as once you pass £1000 you appear on all screens at that university).


Cheers, Nat!


Jack

On Tue, 20 Jan 2026 at 17:09, Jack Thomas < jackthomas@nativecampusadvertising.com> wrote:

Hi Natalie,


Thanks so much for the update on the payment - really appreciated. Apologies for the brain dump you're finding below!


  • We currently project about 8,000 students, but you obviously don't want to be left behind with loads of merchandise, and as with any event, some students will register for a ticket but change their mind on the day, so 5,000 items of merch may be a better figure to aim for.


  • Yes - competitions are a fab way to get students involved, particularly to get followers on your social media and people signed up to a mailing list.


  • Simple competitions e.g. to win free tickets to Prada are a good way to build engagement, but you can always do something a bit more creative. In the past, we had a comedy club that filmed students telling their 'worst dad jokes' on camera for the chance to win a bar tab at one of their shows. This got loads of students up, gave the comedy club loads of social media content, and created a buzz around the stall.


  • You could do a spin on this, and get students to deliver an iconic line from Miranda Priestley herself? Or give them a chance to deliver a line with one of the puppets? You can then film the students engaging with JWP and post the winner online (who then wins free tickets, or a backstage tour, or something?).


  • At these events, other things that work are (yes) merch, and also a spin-the-wheel is often a good way to get engagement, or as you said some sort of activity that students can get involved in.


  • Just thinking out loud, but you could print of pictures of Miranda, Andy, etc and put them on little sticks, and let students go head-to-head to identify which character said which line? With a chance to win something from there (even if it was merch?).


  • Or do something like Cadbury did this year with the Secret Santa Postal Service, and let people nominate their friends / family / theatre lovers in their life for the chance to win? To get people talking?


I've just fired off a few ideas, but let me know what you think. Happy to have another brainstorm if these aren't quite hitting it!


Also just checked your sister out, she's a whole vibe, isn't she! I'm not personally involved in the organisation of the event on that level, but I've put this into a group chat with some people, so I'll let you know what they think.


Thanks,


Jack

subject: Jack & Hadi - Initial Call
body_text:
Hi Hadi, 



Cheers for talking with me just now. 



Just providing a quick recap of our conversation so we've got it on the record. 



You can provide a street-food pop up that's successfully been run at a number of locations, including University of Herts. Your gazebos are 3x3m and you can get a variety of food vendors in, you just require the space and permissions from the university. 



Just to flag we may run into a couple of issues - namely universities wanting to protect their revenue from their on-site catering, and so not allowing third parties to sell food on site, and also that we at native are only really responsible for the student fairs, and so it may be difficult to organise you a separate pop-up outside of these. 



However, I'll do some digging into individual universities and see if we can get a positive response on both points, and come back to you with some options. 



Once we've got the greenlight, we can also arrange a time for you to visit the space and research waste sites, etc. 



Thanks, and will be back shortly, 



Jack Deputy Sales Manager - Campus Advertising - 0204 587 9852

Cheers for talking with me just now.


Just providing a quick recap of our conversation so we've got it on the record.


You can provide a street-food pop up that's successfully been run at a number of locations, including University of Herts. Your gazebos are 3x3m and you can get a variety of food vendors in, you just require the space and permissions from the university.


Just to flag we may run into a couple of issues - namely universities wanting to protect their revenue from their on-site catering, and so not allowing third parties to sell food on site, and also that we at native are only really responsible for the student fairs, and so it may be difficult to organise you a separate pop-up outside of these.


However, I'll do some digging into individual universities and see if we can get a positive response on both points, and come back to you with some options.


Once we've got the greenlight, we can also arrange a time for you to visit the space and research waste sites, etc.


Thanks, and will be back shortly,


Jack

subject: Engage with over 1000 Edinburgh students at Play Your Way Fair on the 5th of Feb
body_text:
Hi there, 


I am contacting you in regards to booking a stall at our upcoming Play Your Way Fair at Edinburgh Napier University on the 5th of February. 



Take advantage of engaging early in the new academic term and connecting directly with thousands of Freshers and returning students in February. 



📅 Date: 5th February 2026 

🕙 Time: 10am – 4pm 

📍 Location: Edinburgh Napier Campus 

👥 Expected Footfall: Over 1000+ students! 



Our Play Your Way Fair is all about engaging with students that like to get into groups, hang out and have an appetite for activities together including gaming, going out for food and any type of entertainment or lifestyle discovery with their new surroundings. 



See how much you can engage on campus this year especially when the students are still looking for the places to go that will form their habits for the next three years. 



Please read the research on this sector if you have time here (Student Life Report 2025–26): https://drive.google.com/file/d/10J04NpnfHQC_wvxs5Eh-w-iM30hnNCbE/view?pli=1. 



If you would like to have a video meeting or a call regarding pricing then please come back to me today 



Kind regards,

: https://meetings-eu1.hubspot.com/aaron-salins?uuid=d71f6227-d330-4680-9294-4912460d93e9 
Aaron Salins
Tel : 01273 257574
Local Sales Manager - Campus Advertising

________________________________
From: Jack Thomas <jackthomas@nativecampusadvertising.com>
Sent: 02 February 2026 10:51
To: Hadi Chraim <hadichraim@hotmail.co.uk>
Subject: Jack & Hadi - Initial Call


Hi Hadi,


Cheers for talking with me just now.


Just providing a quick recap of our conversation so we've got it on the record.


You can provide a street-food pop up that's successfully been run at a number of locations, including University of Herts. Your gazebos are 3x3m and you can get a variety of food vendors in, you just require the space and permissions from the university.


Just to flag we may run into a couple of issues - namely universities wanting to protect their revenue from their on-site catering, and so not allowing third parties to sell food on site, and also that we at native are only really responsible for the student fairs, and so it may be difficult to organise you a separate pop-up outside of these.


However, I'll do some digging into individual universities and see if we can get a positive response on both points, and come back to you with some options.


Once we've got the greenlight, we can also arrange a time for you to visit the space and research waste sites, etc.


Thanks, and will be back shortly,


Jack

subject: Skinnydip - Reaching London Students
body_text:
Hi Megan, Laurina, 



Reaching out as we're currently organising the UK's largest student-only event at the Business Design Centre, on the 7th March. 



We're the official media partner of 12 London universities and we're projecting about 8,000 students to attend the event, with the chance for you to put Skinnydip in front of students studying across the capital. 



Your brand looks super student focused and I know you've worked with us before, so wanted to reach out to see if you were interested? It would give Skinnydip the chance to set up a real-life showroom and engage with thousands of students directly.

subject: Engage with over 1000 Edinburgh students at Play Your Way Fair on the 5th of Feb
body_text:
Hi there, 


I am contacting you in regards to booking a stall at our upcoming Play Your Way Fair at Edinburgh Napier University on the 5th of February. 



Take advantage of engaging early in the new academic term and connecting directly with thousands of Freshers and returning students in February. 



📅 Date: 5th February 2026 

🕙 Time: 10am – 4pm 

📍 Location: Edinburgh Napier Campus 

👥 Expected Footfall: Over 1000+ students! 



Our Play Your Way Fair is all about engaging with students that like to get into groups, hang out and have an appetite for activities together including gaming, going out for food and any type of entertainment or lifestyle discovery with their new surroundings. 



See how much you can engage on campus this year especially when the students are still looking for the places to go that will form their habits for the next three years. 



Please read the research on this sector if you have time here (Student Life Report 2025–26): https://drive.google.com/file/d/10J04NpnfHQC_wvxs5Eh-w-iM30hnNCbE/view?pli=1. 



If you would like to have a video meeting or a call regarding pricing then please come back to me today 



Kind regards,

: https://meetings-eu1.hubspot.com/aaron-salins?uuid=d71f6227-d330-4680-9294-4912460d93e9 
Aaron Salins

subject: Re: Sapphire Seafood Kitchen - Leicester Restaurant - DMU -Welcome Back Fair 10th Feb & Play Your Way Entertainment Fair on 24th Feb
body_text:
Hi Premila, 



Thank you for your email. 



Please see the information below in response to your questions: 



Last year’s event turnout: We don’t have confirmed figures from last year’s event. However, we recently ran a similar event for Welcome Back, which exceeded expectations, and we’re confident we can recreate that level of success again. 



List of participating businesses: The report containing this information is currently being worked on, so I’m unable to access it at the moment. As soon as it’s available, I’ll be happy to share the details with you. Alternatively, you’re welcome to speak directly with the Service Delivery team, who will also be supporting you on the day. 



Student emails with your branding: Student Union emails typically go out 5–7 days before the event, followed by a further email 5–7 days after the event, as I’ve been advised. 



Social media posts with your branding: Social media promotion usually begins around 14-12 days before the event. This means it may be missed for the Welcome Back Fair, but will be included for the Play Your Fair. The price you’ve been quoted (£1,150) covers both fairs and is less than the standard rate for Activate Plus, which does not normally include any digital elements. For this reason, we would ideally need to secure this as soon as possible so we can prepare everything in time for you. 



Banner measurements 

Please note the banner specifications below: 



Dimensions: 800 × 2000mm 

Preferred formats: .eps or .svg 

Alternative formats: .jpg, .png, .gif, or .pdf 

Graphics should be supplied as soon as possible, with a minimum deadline of two weeks before the event. Please ensure your image assets are ready as soon as possible so the Service Delivery team can arrange printing in good time. 



If you have any questions or need anything further, please don’t hesitate to let me know. 



Kind regards 

Sat Singh

body_html:
Hi Premila,


Thank you for your email.


Please see the information below in response to your questions:


Last year’s event turnout: We don’t have confirmed figures from last year’s event. However, we recently ran a similar event for Welcome Back, which exceeded expectations, and we’re confident we can recreate that level of success again.


List of participating businesses: The report containing this information is currently being worked on, so I’m unable to access it at the moment. As soon as it’s available, I’ll be happy to share the details with you. Alternatively, you’re welcome to speak directly with the Service Delivery team, who will also be supporting you on the day.


Student emails with your branding: Student Union emails typically go out 5–7 days before the event, followed by a further email 5–7 days after the event, as I’ve been advised.


Social media posts with your branding: Social media promotion usually begins around 14-12 days before the event. This means it may be missed for the Welcome Back Fair, but will be included for the Play Your Fair. The price you’ve been quoted (£1,150) covers both fairs and is less than the standard rate for Activate Plus, which does not normally include any digital elements. For this reason, we would ideally need to secure this as soon as possible so we can prepare everything in time for you.


Banner measurements

Please note the banner specifications below:


  • Dimensions: 800 × 2000mm

  • Preferred formats: .eps or .svg

  • Alternative formats: .jpg, .png, .gif, or .pdf

Graphics should be supplied as soon as possible, with a minimum deadline of two weeks before the event. Please ensure your image assets are ready as soon as possible so the Service Delivery team can arrange printing in good time.


If you have any questions or need anything further, please don’t hesitate to let me know.


Kind regards

Sat Singh


Satvir Singh - Local Bookings Manager - Campus Advertising - 01339 26 42 73

subject: Connect with 1,200 students+ face-to-face at De Montfort University
body_text:
Hi Secret Garden Restaurant & Lounge, 



I hope you’re well. 



I work with the Students’ Union at De Montfort University, helping businesses connect face-to-face with students on campus through high-footfall events. 



We’re expecting around 1,200+ students on the day, giving you the opportunity to: 



Speak directly with students (not just online ads) 

Build trust and brand awareness with a new audience 

Drive immediate sign-ups, bookings, or sales 

Position your brand early with students new to Leicester 

Secret Garden stood out as a great fit, so I wanted to share a few upcoming opportunities: 



Upcoming on-campus fairs: 



Welcome Back: Refreshers Fair – 10 February 2026 | Location: SU Building 

Play Your Way: Entertainment Fair – 24 February 2026 | Location: Campus Centre 

Feel Good: Sports & Fitness – 24 March 2026 | Location: Campus Centre 

Feel Good: Health & Wellness – 21 April 2026 |Location: Campus Centre 

To show the real impact, here’s a short slide deck with data and examples from businesses who’ve attended previously, including engagement levels and why many rebook year after year: 👉 [Data-Driven Benefits for Brand Awareness & ROI]: https://docs.google.com/presentation/d/1kWnF0WZB-WebBTFAle4Oe74hoQlxHxyMG6poPxzMe3U/edit?usp=sharing 



Spaces are limited and allocated on a first-come basis. 



If this is of interest, the next step is simply to reply “Yes” and I can: 



Share pricing and availability 

Hold a provisional space for you 

Or arrange a quick 10-minute call to see if it’s a good fit 

Looking forward to hearing from you. 



Best regards, 



Sat Singh 

Satvir Singh - Local Bookings Manager - Campus Advertising - 01339 26 42 73

subject: Connect with 1,000 students+ face-to-face at Napier Edinburgh University
body_text:
Hi Winstons, 



I hope you’re well. 



I work with the Students’ Union at Napier Edinburgh University, helping businesses connect face-to-face with students on campus through high-footfall events. 



We’re expecting around 1,000+ students on the day, giving you the opportunity to: 



Speak directly with students (not just online ads) 

Build trust and brand awareness with a new audience 

Drive immediate sign-ups, bookings, or sales 

Position your brand early with students new to Edinburgh 

Winstons stood out as a great fit, so I wanted to share a few upcoming opportunities: 



Upcoming on-campus fairs: 



Play Your Way: Entertainment Fair – 5th February 2026 | Location: Craiglockhart Campus: Foyer: https://drive.google.com/file/d/13e8vFTC3_KLwWiMw1PEXRdjdjwY1uCMl/view?usp=sharing 

To show the real impact, here’s a short slide deck with data and examples from businesses who’ve attended previously, including engagement levels and why many rebook year after year: 👉 [Data-Driven Benefits for Brand Awareness & ROI]: https://docs.google.com/presentation/d/1kWnF0WZB-WebBTFAle4Oe74hoQlxHxyMG6poPxzMe3U/edit?usp=sharing 



Spaces are limited and allocated on a first-come basis. 



If this is of interest, the next step is simply to reply “Yes” and I can: 



Share pricing and availability 

Hold a provisional space for you 

Or arrange a quick 10-minute call to see if it’s a good fit 

Looking forward to hearing from you. 



Best regards, 



Sat Singh 

Satvir Singh - Local Bookings Manager - Campus Advertising - 01339 26 42 73

subject: Engage with over 1000 Edinburgh students at Play Your Way Fair on the 5th of Feb
body_text:
Hi there, 


I am contacting you in regards to booking a stall at our upcoming Play Your Way Fair at Edinburgh Napier University on the 5th of February. 



Take advantage of engaging early in the new academic term and connecting directly with thousands of Freshers and returning students in February. 



📅 Date: 5th February 2026 

🕙 Time: 10am – 4pm 

📍 Location: Edinburgh Napier Campus 

👥 Expected Footfall: Over 1000+ students! 



Our Play Your Way Fair is all about engaging with students that like to get into groups, hang out and have an appetite for activities together including gaming, going out for food and any type of entertainment or lifestyle discovery with their new surroundings. 



See how much you can engage on campus this year especially when the students are still looking for the places to go that will form their habits for the next three years. 



Please read the research on this sector if you have time here (Student Life Report 2025–26): https://drive.google.com/file/d/10J04NpnfHQC_wvxs5Eh-w-iM30hnNCbE/view?pli=1. 



If you would like to have a video meeting or a call regarding pricing then please come back to me today 



Kind regards,

I am contacting you in regards to booking a stall at our upcoming Play Your Way Fair at Edinburgh Napier University on the 5th of February.


Take advantage of engaging early in the new academic term and connecting directly with thousands of Freshers and returning students in February.

📅 Date: 5th February 2026

🕙 Time: 10am – 4pm

📍 Location: Edinburgh Napier Campus

👥 Expected Footfall: Over 1000+ students!


Our Play Your Way Fair is all about engaging with students that like to get into groups, hang out and have an appetite for activities together including gaming, going out for food and any type of entertainment or lifestyle discovery with their new surroundings.


See how much you can engage on campus this year especially when the students are still looking for the places to go that will form their habits for the next three years.


Please read the research on this sector if you have time here (Student Life Report 2025–26).


If you would like to have a video meeting or a call regarding pricing then please come back to me today



Kind regards,


Aaron Salins
Tel : 01273 257574
Local Sales Manager - Campus Advertising


partner.native.fm | LinkedIn

subject: WeBuyBooks - Reaching Uni Students
body_text:
Hi Brad, 



Reaching out as we're the official media partner of approximately 50% of the Students Unions in the UK, and I know university students regularly have higher-value books that they no longer need at the end of each semester. 



We've got a number of ways we can promote you to the students, including email, social media, digital screens in universities, and on-campus events - where you can come and reach students face-to-face in their thousands, all in one day, and introduce them to your service. 



Would be keen to get a Google Meet in with you to discuss. 



Thanks, 



Jack 

Deputy Sales Manager - Campus Advertising - 0204 587 9852

Office address: Floor 2, 91 – 93 Great Eastern Street, Shoreditch, London EC2A 3HZ. 
Registered Office: 30 Bankside Court, Stationfields, Kidlington, Oxford, England, OX5 1JE. 
Co. No: 09729773 | VAT: 255631209

subject: Connect with 1,000 students+ face-to-face at Napier Edinburgh University
body_text:
Hi The Blackbird, 



I hope you’re well. 



I work with the Students’ Union at Napier Edinburgh University, helping businesses connect face-to-face with students on campus through high-footfall events. 



We’re expecting around 1,000+ students on the day, giving you the opportunity to: 



Speak directly with students (not just online ads) 

Build trust and brand awareness with a new audience 

Drive immediate sign-ups, bookings, or sales 

Position your brand early with students new to Edinburgh 

The Blackbird Bar and Restaurant stood out as a great fit, so I wanted to share a few upcoming opportunities: 



Upcoming on-campus fairs: 



Play Your Way: Entertainment Fair – 5th February 2026 | Location: Craiglockhart Campus: Foyer: https://drive.google.com/file/d/13e8vFTC3_KLwWiMw1PEXRdjdjwY1uCMl/view?usp=sharing 

To show the real impact, here’s a short slide deck with data and examples from businesses who’ve attended previously, including engagement levels and why many rebook year after year: 👉 [Data-Driven Benefits for Brand Awareness & ROI]: https://docs.google.com/presentation/d/1kWnF0WZB-WebBTFAle4Oe74hoQlxHxyMG6poPxzMe3U/edit?usp=sharing 



Spaces are limited and allocated on a first-come basis. 



If this is of interest, the next step is simply to reply “Yes” and I can: 



Share pricing and availability 

Hold a provisional space for you 

Or arrange a quick 10-minute call to see if it’s a good fit 

Looking forward to hearing from you. 



Best regards, 



Sat Singh 

Satvir Singh - Local Bookings Manager - Campus Advertising - 01339 26 42 73

: https://meetings-eu1.hubspot.com/satvir

Hi Holly,

Please find the answers to your questions below:

  1. Install packs for the site

Leeds:

Pitch name  - The Precinct

Pitch location – full location and postcode - Leeds University Union, Lifton Place, LS2 9JZ

Maps Link or picture - https://www.google.com/maps/place/Lifton+Pl,+Woodhouse,+Leeds+LS2+9JZ/@53.80717,-1.5571445,106m/data=!3m1!1e3!4m6!3m5!1s0x48795ea94cff1f33:0x97bf2c34a7f4d0d2!8m2!3d53.8073638!4d-1.5583667!16s%2Fm%2F010rq5z5?entry=ttu&g_ep=EgoyMDI0MTAyMC4xIKXMDSoASAFQAw%3D%3D 

Instructions to access the Pitch location - Access Precinct from here  Unload at the spot of the activation and remove the vehicle before 9am.


Loading details (how far is the loading location to the pitch) - Entry to the Precinct is via Leeds University Union, Lifton Place, LS2 9JZ - google maps link is here. You can access from the corner of Lifton Place and Cromer Terrace
You will stop at some bollards (images below). Security will need the registration of your van in advance so they can let you in - please confirm the reg number of the hire van as soon as you have it. The SU contacts will also help you on the day
Note that you must drive at 5mph maximum and have your hazards on at all time when moving through the precinct. A member of your team should act as a banksman and walk in front of the van, preferably in a hi-vis jacket.
 
Please note that you cannot move vehicles around the Precinct between the hours of 9am and 5pm. Failure to observe this rule could result in a fine.
As such, please aim to arrive at 8am, or at a time which gives you plenty of wriggle room to unload and move your van comfortably before 9am.
You can continue to set up your stand after 9am, you just won't have access to the vehicle.

Parking - Is available on campus. No permit is needed, but we need to register your vehicle at least one week in advance. This is for both accessing the campus and parking


Additional parking for reps -  8am set up time 

SU contacts - 0113 380 1219 you can ask for Alex  or Ritwija


  1. Confirmation of the live timings 1pm - 5pm
Yes that is fine 

  1. Can the booth be loaded in at 8.30am ready to be in place for 10am, covered by cladding, ready for the reveal at 1pm.

Yes this should be fine will share this with the su now.

  1. The exact location address for our site space.

Will be on the precinct

Leeds University Union, Lifton Place, LS2 9JZ


  1. Anything additional I need to complete my side for the booking

We need your PLI and RA as soon as possible
We also need the registration of the van which is unloading on the day and please let me know if this van also needs parking.
If power is required please let me know.
And attendees' names and phone numbers would be great also.

I hope this helps!

As this booking has only just come through, there may be a few questions from the SU's side, in which case I will reach out; however, for now, all that we need is mentioned above.

Many thanks,
Esme

—
Esme Cowan

subject: Engage with over 1000 Edinburgh students at Play Your Way Fair on the 5th of Feb
body_text:
Hi Georgie, 


I am contacting you in regards to booking a stall at our upcoming Play Your Way Fair at Edinburgh Napier University on the 5th of February. 



Take advantage of engaging early in the new academic term and connecting directly with thousands of Freshers and returning students in February. 


📅 Date: 5th February 2026 

🕙 Time: 10am – 4pm 

📍 Location: Edinburgh Napier Campus 

👥 Expected Footfall: Over 1000+ students! 



Our Play Your Way Fair is all about engaging with students that like to get into groups, hang out and have an appetite for activities together including gaming, going out for food and any type of entertainment or lifestyle discovery with their new surroundings. 



See how much you can engage on campus this year especially when the students are still looking for the places to go that will form their habits for the next three years. 



Please read the research on this sector if you have time here (Student Life Report 2025–26): https://drive.google.com/file/d/10J04NpnfHQC_wvxs5Eh-w-iM30hnNCbE/view?pli=1. 



If you would like to have a video meeting or a call regarding pricing then please come back to me today 

Kind regards,

: https://meetings-eu1.hubspot.com/aaron-salins?uuid=d71f6227-d330-4680-9294-4912460d93e9 
Aaron Salins
Tel : 01273 257574
Local Sales Manager - Campus Advertising

subject: Connect with 1,000 students+ face-to-face at Napier Edinburgh University
body_text:
Hi Lisa, 



I hope you’re well. 



I work with the Students’ Union at Napier Edinburgh University, helping businesses connect face-to-face with students on campus through high-footfall events. 



We’re expecting around 1,000+ students on the day, giving you the opportunity to: 



Speak directly with students (not just online ads) 

Build trust and brand awareness with a new audience 

Drive immediate sign-ups, bookings, or sales 

Position your brand early with students new to Edinburgh 

Diageo stood out as a great fit, so I wanted to share a few upcoming opportunities: 



Upcoming on-campus fairs: 



Play Your Way: Entertainment Fair – 5th February 2026 | Location: Craiglockhart Campus: Foyer: https://drive.google.com/file/d/13e8vFTC3_KLwWiMw1PEXRdjdjwY1uCMl/view?usp=sharing 

To show the real impact, here’s a short slide deck with data and examples from businesses who’ve attended previously, including engagement levels and why many rebook year after year: 👉 [Data-Driven Benefits for Brand Awareness & ROI]: https://docs.google.com/presentation/d/1kWnF0WZB-WebBTFAle4Oe74hoQlxHxyMG6poPxzMe3U/edit?usp=sharing 



Spaces are limited and allocated on a first-come basis. 



If this is of interest, the next step is simply to reply “Yes” and I can: 



Share pricing and availability 

Hold a provisional space for you 

Or arrange a quick 10-minute call to see if it’s a good fit 

Looking forward to hearing from you. 



Best regards, 



Sat Singh 

Satvir Singh - Local Bookings Manager - Campus Advertising - 01339 26 42 73

: https://meetings-eu1.hubspot.com/satvir

subject: Edinburgh Napier - Play Your Way Fair
body_text:
Hi Volunteer Scotland 



My Name is Neil, I'm reaching out from Native. We are the official media partner for a large number of universities across the UK. 

I am reaching out with a last-minute opportunity to attend a fair at Edinburgh Napier. This fair is designed to introduce the students to different events & activities which they can do within the City of Edinburgh. Volunteering in their friend groups would be a great activity for them to get behind. 


I've attached a PDF to give you all the information regarding the upcoming fair at Edinburgh Napier, which is being held at the Craiglockhart Campus on the 5th of February. 




You will be able to see all the different options we provide at the Fair, along with the standard pricing, which excludes VAT. 




As you are a charity & the work which you provide, we can provide you with a Stall at the event for as little as £200 (+VAT). This will give you the chance to market directly to over 1000 Students at the Student Union. 




Many Thanks, 

Neil Eggar --
: https://meetings-eu1.hubspot.com/meetings/Neil-Eggar 

Neil Eggar (he/him)
Sales Manager

Tel: 01224 980141


Powering campus culture

subject: Campus Promotional Fairs - Napier Uni
body_text:
Hi team, 



I hope you don't mind me reaching out, I work within the F&B team at Native - the company who handle the campus fairs within Edinburgh Napier University. 



Currently in process of introducing the team and providing information on such campus fairs to all relevant businesses in the area - I understand a colleague may have been in touch today. 



An advertiser space has became available for the upcoming student event this Thursday following a cancellation, would this be something the team would be interested in taking over? Prices and necessary information can be found inside attached file - note that prices will be discounted 25% from the listed RRP due to the lead time until the event. 



If there are issues that permit you from assessing, booking or delivering this event, do let me know as the university is keen to address such reasonings in upcoming events. Additionally, if this would be something of use to your Edinburgh store at a later date, please do notify us. 



Best, 

Indy

subject: Edinburgh Napier - Student Fair' Play Your Way
body_text:
Hi SAHM Team, 



My Name is Neil, I'm reaching out from Native. We are the official media partner for a large number of universities across the UK. 

I am reaching out with a last-minute opportunity to attend a fair at Edinburgh Napier. This fair is designed to introduce the students to different events, activities and ways to unwind coming into exam time within the City of Edinburgh. Given the stress that students are facing at this time of the academic year, this would be the perfect time to introduce them to SAHM face-to-face. 


I've attached a PDF to give you all the information regarding the upcoming fair at Edinburgh Napier, which is being held at the Craiglockhart Campus on the 5th of February. 




You will be able to see all the different options we provide at the Fair, along with the standard pricing, which excludes VAT. 




As you are a charity & the work which you provide, we can provide you with a Stall at the event for as little as £200 (+VAT). This will give you the chance to market directly to over 1000 Students at the Student Union. 




Many Thanks, 

Neil Eggar --
: https://meetings-eu1.hubspot.com/meetings/Neil-Eggar 

Neil Eggar (he/him)
Sales Manager

Tel: 01224 980141


Powering campus culture

subject: att. Katie - late booking for Feb 5th, Edinburgh Napier - Play Your Way
body_text:
Hi Katie, 



I know we've reached out a few times in the past but have not as yet been able to make contact with you. We operate as the media partner for Edinburgh Napier University. 



On Feb 5 (this Thursday!) we've got the Play Your Way campus fair taking place all day, with around 1,100 students due to attend. This fair is specifically themed around entertainment, social activity, and typically drives group bookings to local businesses who come to the day and promote their brand through a stall or event space. 



We'd like to confirm (and I appreciate this is very late notice) if you would be free to come on Thursday and promote the cinema to the students in attendance. 



Whilst we cannot offer complimentary spaces as this is a powerful marketing opportunity, we do understand this is short notice and are happy to be flexible on the rates should you be available this week. 



Please do give me a call on my remote number 0114 352 0799 asap if you are open to exploring this opportunity! 



Best, 



Matt Billington (he/him)

subject: D4100 - Uni Students
body_text:
Hi Jake, Jacob, 



Long time I know! We were speaking before about bringing D4100 to Freshers Fairs. 



We're now organising a centralised event that attracts students from 12 London universities across the capital. 



We're expecting approx 8000 students to attend, so it's a pretty big deal, and I know you guys had some cool ideas before so if you were still keen to promote your events etc to students, this would be a fab stage to do it on. 



Have you got a quick 15 mins for a call this week to run it past you? 



Thanks, 



Jack Deputy Sales Manager - Campus Advertising - 0204 587 9852

subject: FOA GM: Connect with 1,000 students+ face-to-face at Napier Edinburgh University
body_text:
Hi GM, 



I hope you’re well. 



I work with the Students’ Union at Napier Edinburgh University, helping businesses connect face-to-face with students on campus through high-footfall events. 



We’re expecting around 1,000+ students on the day, giving you the opportunity to: 



Speak directly with students (not just online ads) 

Build trust and brand awareness with a new audience 

Drive immediate sign-ups, bookings, or sales 

Position your brand early with students new to Edinburgh 

Panda & Sons stood out as a great fit, so I wanted to share a few upcoming opportunities: 



Upcoming on-campus fairs: 



Play Your Way: Entertainment Fair – 5th February 2026 | Location: Craiglockhart Campus: Foyer: https://drive.google.com/file/d/13e8vFTC3_KLwWiMw1PEXRdjdjwY1uCMl/view?usp=sharing 

To show the real impact, here’s a short slide deck with data and examples from businesses who’ve attended previously, including engagement levels and why many rebook year after year: 👉 [Data-Driven Benefits for Brand Awareness & ROI]: https://docs.google.com/presentation/d/1kWnF0WZB-WebBTFAle4Oe74hoQlxHxyMG6poPxzMe3U/edit?usp=sharing 



Spaces are limited and allocated on a first-come basis. 



If this is of interest, the next step is simply to reply “Yes” and I can: 



Share pricing and availability 

Hold a provisional space for you 

Or arrange a quick 10-minute call to see if it’s a good fit 

Looking forward to hearing from you. 



Best regards, 



Sat Singh 

Satvir Singh - Local Bookings Manager - Campus Advertising - 01339 26 42 73

subject: Edinburgh Napier - Student Fair' Play your Way
body_text:
Hi Change Mental Health Team, 



My Name is Neil, I'm reaching out from Native. We are the official media partner for a large number of universities across the UK. 

I am reaching out with a last-minute opportunity to attend a fair at Edinburgh Napier. This fair is designed to introduce the students to different events, activities and ways to unwind coming into exam time within the City of Edinburgh. Given the stress that students are facing at this time of the academic year, this would be the perfect time to introduce them to Change Mental Health face-to-face. 



I've attached a PDF to give you all the information regarding the upcoming fair at Edinburgh Napier, which is being held at the Craiglockhart Campus on the 5th of February. 



You will be able to see all the different options we provide at the Fair, along with the standard pricing, which excludes VAT. 



As you are a charity & the work which you provide, we can provide you with a Stall at the event for as little as £200 (+VAT). This will give you the chance to market directly to over 1000 Students at the Student Union. 



Many Thanks, 

Neil Eggar --
: https://meetings-eu1.hubspot.com/meetings/Neil-Eggar 

Neil Eggar (he/him)
Sales Manager

Tel: 01224 980141


Powering campus culture
partner.native.fm: https://partner.native.fm/?utm_source=signature&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=brand | LinkedIn: https://www.linkedin.com/company/nativecampus/ 

Office address: 28 Brunswick Place, London, N1 6DZ. Registered Office: Pavilion View, Brighton, BN1 1EY. Registered as a company in England and Wales, number: 09729773. VAT Registration Number: 255631209.

Hi Holly,

Please find the answers to your questions below:


  1.
Install packs for the site

Leeds:

Pitch name  - The Precinct

Pitch location – full location and postcode - Leeds University Union, Lifton Place, LS2 9JZ

Maps Link or picture - <https://www.google.com/maps/place/Lifton+Pl,+Woodhouse,+Leeds+LS2+9JZ/@53.80717,-1.5571445,106m/data=!3m1!1e3!4m6!3m5!1s0x48795ea94cff1f33:0x97bf2c34a7f4d0d2!8m2!3d53.8073638!4d-1.5583667!16s%2Fm%2F010rq5z5?entry=ttu&g_ep=EgoyMDI0MTAyMC4xIKXMDSoASAFQAw%3D%3D> https://www.google.com/maps/place/Lifton+Pl,+Woodhouse,+Leeds+LS2+9JZ/@53.80717,-1.5571445,106m/data=!3m1!1e3!4m6!3m5!1s0x48795ea94cff1f33:0x97bf2c34a7f4d0d2!8m2!3d53.8073638!4d-1.5583667!16s%2Fm%2F010rq5z5?entry=ttu&g_ep=EgoyMDI0MTAyMC4xIKXMDSoASAFQAw%3D%3D

Instructions to access the Pitch location -Access Precinct from <https://maps.app.goo.gl/uFZJwWFn7ZD6eZEh9> here<https://maps.app.goo.gl/uFZJwWFn7ZD6eZEh9> Unload at the spot of the activation and remove the vehicle before 9am.


Loading details (how far is the loading location to the pitch) -Entry to the Precinct is via Leeds University Union, Lifton Place, LS2 9JZ - google maps link is here. You can access from the corner of Lifton Place and Cromer Terrace
You will stop at some bollards (images below). Security will need the registration of your van in advance so they can let you in - please confirm the reg number of the hire van as soon as you have it. The SU contacts will also help you on the day
Note that you must drive at 5mph maximum and have your hazards on at all time when moving through the precinct. A member of your team should act as a banksman and walk in front of the van, preferably in a hi-vis jacket.

Please note that you cannot move vehicles around the Precinct between the hours of 9am and 5pm. Failure to observe this rule could result in a fine.
As such, please aim to arrive at 8am, or at a time which gives you plenty of wriggle room to unload and move your van comfortably before 9am.
You can continue to set up your stand after 9am, you just won't have access to the vehicle.

Parking - Is available on campus. No permit is needed, but we need to register your vehicle at least one week in advance. This is for both accessing the campus and parking


Additional parking for reps - 8am set up time

SU contacts - 0113 380 1219 you can ask for Alex or Ritwija



  1.
Confirmation of the live timings 1pm - 5pm

Yes that is fine


  1.
Can the booth be loaded in at 8.30am ready to be in place for 10am, covered by cladding, ready for the reveal at 1pm.

Yes this should be fine will share this with the su now.


  1.
The exact location address for our site space.

Will be on the precinct

Leeds University Union, Lifton Place, LS2 9JZ



  1.
Anything additional I need to complete my side for the booking

We need your PLI and RA as soon as possible
We also need the registration of the van which is unloading on the day and please let me know if this van also needs parking.
If power is required please let me know.
And attendees' names and phone numbers would be great also.

I hope this helps!

As this booking has only just come through, there may be a few questions from the SU's side, in which case I will reach out; however, for now, all that we need is mentioned above.

Many thanks,
Esme

Hi Holly,

Please find the answers to your questions below:

  1. Install packs for the site

Leeds:

Pitch name  - The Precinct

Pitch location – full location and postcode - Leeds University Union, Lifton Place, LS2 9JZ

Maps Link or picture - https://www.google.com/maps/place/Lifton+Pl,+Woodhouse,+Leeds+LS2+9JZ/@53.80717,-1.5571445,106m/data=!3m1!1e3!4m6!3m5!1s0x48795ea94cff1f33:0x97bf2c34a7f4d0d2!8m2!3d53.8073638!4d-1.5583667!16s%2Fm%2F010rq5z5?entry=ttu&g_ep=EgoyMDI0MTAyMC4xIKXMDSoASAFQAw%3D%3D 

Instructions to access the Pitch location - Access Precinct from here  Unload at the spot of the activation and remove the vehicle before 9am.


Loading details (how far is the loading location to the pitch) - Entry to the Precinct is via Leeds University Union, Lifton Place, LS2 9JZ - google maps link is here. You can access from the corner of Lifton Place and Cromer Terrace
You will stop at some bollards (images below). Security will need the registration of your van in advance so they can let you in - please confirm the reg number of the hire van as soon as you have it. The SU contacts will also help you on the day
Note that you must drive at 5mph maximum and have your hazards on at all time when moving through the precinct. A member of your team should act as a banksman and walk in front of the van, preferably in a hi-vis jacket.
 
Please note that you cannot move vehicles around the Precinct between the hours of 9am and 5pm. Failure to observe this rule could result in a fine.
As such, please aim to arrive at 8am, or at a time which gives you plenty of wriggle room to unload and move your van comfortably before 9am.
You can continue to set up your stand after 9am, you just won't have access to the vehicle.

Parking - Is available on campus. No permit is needed, but we need to register your vehicle at least one week in advance. This is for both accessing the campus and parking


Additional parking for reps -  8am set up time 

SU contacts - 0113 380 1219 you can ask for Alex  or Ritwija


  1. Confirmation of the live timings 1pm - 5pm
Yes that is fine 

  1. Can the booth be loaded in at 8.30am ready to be in place for 10am, covered by cladding, ready for the reveal at 1pm.

Yes this should be fine will share this with the su now.

  1. The exact location address for our site space.

Will be on the precinct

Leeds University Union, Lifton Place, LS2 9JZ


  1. Anything additional I need to complete my side for the booking

We need your PLI and RA as soon as possible
We also need the registration of the van which is unloading on the day and please let me know if this van also needs parking.
If power is required please let me know.
And attendees' names and phone numbers would be great also.

I hope this helps!

As this booking has only just come through, there may be a few questions from the SU's side, in which case I will reach out; however, for now, all that we need is mentioned above.

Many thanks,
Esme

—

subject: Engaging with Students on campus
body_text:
Hi there, 

Just checking back in as we continue to confirm spaces for the upcoming student fair. 

These events work really well for businesses looking to connect with students early, build brand awareness, and drive footfall or future sales. Students are actively discovering new brands on campus, especially at this point in the term. 

If it’s helpful, I’m happy to suggest what usually works best for similar businesses and keep things flexible around setup and pricing. 

Let me know if you’d like to have a quick chat. 

Best, 
Kieran

: https://meetings-eu1.hubspot.com/kieran-berry-campbell

subject: Edinburgh Napier - Student Fair' Play Your Way
body_text:
Hi Who Cares Team, 



My Name is Neil, I'm reaching out from Native. We are the official media partner for a large number of universities across the UK. 

I am reaching out with a last-minute opportunity to attend a fair at Edinburgh Napier. This fair is designed to introduce the students to different events, activities and ways to unwind coming into exam time within the City of Edinburgh. Given the stress that students are facing at this time of the academic year, this would be the perfect time to introduce them to Who Cares? Scotland face-to-face. 



I've attached a PDF to give you all the information regarding the upcoming fair at Edinburgh Napier, which is being held at the Craiglockhart Campus on the 5th of February. 



You will be able to see all the different options we provide at the Fair, along with the standard pricing, which excludes VAT. 



As you are a charity & the work which you provide, we can provide you with a Stall at the event for as little as £200 (+VAT). This will give you the chance to market directly to over 1000 Students at the Student Union. 



Many Thanks, 

Neil Eggar --
: https://meetings-eu1.hubspot.com/meetings/Neil-Eggar 

Neil Eggar (he/him)
Sales Manager

Tel: 01224 980141


Powering campus culture

subject: Connect with 1,000 students+ face-to-face at Napier Edinburgh University
body_text:
Hi Claire, 



I hope you’re well. 



I work with the Students’ Union at Napier Edinburgh University, helping businesses connect face-to-face with students on campus through high-footfall events. 



We’re expecting around 1,000+ students on the day, giving you the opportunity to: 



Speak directly with students (not just online ads) 

Build trust and brand awareness with a new audience 

Drive immediate sign-ups, bookings, or sales 

Position your brand early with students new to Edinburgh 

stood out as a great fit, so I wanted to share a few upcoming opportunities: 



Upcoming on-campus fairs: 



Play Your Way: Entertainment Fair – 5th February 2026 | Location: Craiglockhart Campus: Foyer: https://drive.google.com/file/d/13e8vFTC3_KLwWiMw1PEXRdjdjwY1uCMl/view?usp=sharing 

To show the real impact, here’s a short slide deck with data and examples from businesses who’ve attended previously, including engagement levels and why many rebook year after year: 👉 [Data-Driven Benefits for Brand Awareness & ROI]: https://docs.google.com/presentation/d/1kWnF0WZB-WebBTFAle4Oe74hoQlxHxyMG6poPxzMe3U/edit?usp=sharing 



Spaces are limited and allocated on a first-come basis. 



If this is of interest, the next step is simply to reply “Yes” and I can: 



Share pricing and availability 

Hold a provisional space for you 

Or arrange a quick 10-minute call to see if it’s a good fit 

Looking forward to hearing from you. 



Best regards, 



Sat Singh 

Satvir Singh - Local Bookings Manager - Campus Advertising - 01339 26 42 73

: https://meetings-eu1.hubspot.com/satvir

  • Play Your Way: Entertainment Fair – 5th February 2026 | Location: Craiglockhart Campus: Foyer

To show the real impact, here’s a short slide deck with data and examples from businesses who’ve attended previously, including engagement levels and why many rebook year after year: 👉 [Data-Driven Benefits for Brand Awareness & ROI]


Spaces are limited and allocated on a first-come basis.


If this is of interest, the next step is simply to reply “Yes” and I can:


  • Share pricing and availability

  • Hold a provisional space for you

  • Or arrange a quick 10-minute call to see if it’s a good fit

Looking forward to hearing from you.


Best regards,


Sat Singh

subject: Connect with 1,000 students+ face-to-face at Napier Edinburgh University
body_text:
Hi Lucky Liquor, 



I hope you’re well. 



I work with the Students’ Union at Napier Edinburgh University, helping businesses connect face-to-face with students on campus through high-footfall events. 



We’re expecting around 1,000+ students on the day, giving you the opportunity to: 



Speak directly with students (not just online ads) 

Build trust and brand awareness with a new audience 

Drive immediate sign-ups, bookings, or sales 

Position your brand early with students new to Edinburgh 

Lucky Liquor Co. stood out as a great fit, so I wanted to share a few upcoming opportunities: 



Upcoming on-campus fairs: 



Play Your Way: Entertainment Fair – 5th February 2026 | Location: Craiglockhart Campus: Foyer: https://drive.google.com/file/d/13e8vFTC3_KLwWiMw1PEXRdjdjwY1uCMl/view?usp=sharing 

To show the real impact, here’s a short slide deck with data and examples from businesses who’ve attended previously, including engagement levels and why many rebook year after year: 👉 [Data-Driven Benefits for Brand Awareness & ROI]: https://docs.google.com/presentation/d/1kWnF0WZB-WebBTFAle4Oe74hoQlxHxyMG6poPxzMe3U/edit?usp=sharing 



Spaces are limited and allocated on a first-come basis. 



If this is of interest, the next step is simply to reply “Yes” and I can: 



Share pricing and availability 

Hold a provisional space for you 

Or arrange a quick 10-minute call to see if it’s a good fit 

Looking forward to hearing from you. 



Best regards, 



Sat Singh 

Satvir Singh - Local Bookings Manager - Campus Advertising - 01339 26 42 73

: https://meetings-eu1.hubspot.com/satvir
